name: CryptoAutoPost — X Metrics Puller
description: Every 2 hours, reads recent tweet IDs from a Make.com Data Store, fetches public_metrics (impressions/likes/retweets) via the X API v2, and POSTs them to the local metrics receiver (src/agents/metrics_receiver.py) which writes queue/<date>_<slot>/metrics.json and rebuilds the analytics dashboard. Import this into Make.com via 'Create a new scenario' → 'Import blueprint'.

flow._comment_timer: Adjust interval to taste. X API v2 free tier allows ~1 read per 15s; this scenario stays well below that.

datastore.label: CryptoAutoPost_Tweets (create with columns: tweet_id, date, slot, posted_at)

flow._comment_datastore: In Make.com → Data Stores → Add. Schema: tweet_id (text), date (text YYYY-MM-DD), slot (text), posted_at (date). Populate by adding a DataStore:AddRecord module to x_publisher.json after CreateATweet (see README).

flow._comment_auth: Paste your X API v2 App Bearer Token into Make's scenario variables as X_BEARER_TOKEN (read-only is enough). Or replace with an HTTP:OAuth2 module bound to your X connection.

flow._comment_shape: Builds the JSON body the receiver expects: {date, slot, x: {impressions, likes, retweets}}.

flow._comment_receiver: Expose metrics_receiver.py over the internet (e.g. via `ngrok http 8787`) and paste the public URL as METRICS_RECEIVER_URL. The Bearer token must equal METRICS_WEBHOOK_SECRET in your .env.

metadata.notes: Prerequisites: (1) create Data Store 'CryptoAutoPost_Tweets' with columns tweet_id|date|slot|posted_at; (2) scenario env vars X_BEARER_TOKEN + METRICS_RECEIVER_URL + METRICS_WEBHOOK_SECRET; (3) run metrics_receiver.py locally and expose with ngrok. To populate the Data Store, add a DataStore:AddRecord step to x_publisher.json (see make_blueprints/README.md).
